programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff hear about the dangers of fishing for Bluefin Tuna in the Atlantic Ocean from locals in Gloucester, Massachusetts. Then they head 30 miles out onto the open ocean with Darrin of Reel Respect Charters in the hopes of landing their own giant.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff team up with their friends from Boss Shotshells in South Dakota for a high-energy upland hunt, pursuing wild pheasants with the help of skilled hunting dogs. The adventure takes a quirky turn with a visit to the iconic Corn Palace.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark continues his quest in New Zealand, but this time, after a Wapiti. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide, Shawn. Elk were first introduced to New Zealand in 1909 when President Roosevelt gifted a herd of 20 animals that were released on the South Island. Get ready to immerse yourself in the beauty of nature that New Zealand has to offer.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark is wrapping up his Argentina hunt and heading to Entre Rios to track down the final two birds on his list: the Rock Pigeon and the Eared Dove. This area is home to over 10 million birds, making it a prime hunting ground. With flocks of pigeons and doves filling the skies, Mark is set for a fast-paced, action-packed finish to his hunt—a perfect end to an unforgettable adventure.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson sets his sights on a Free-Range New Zealand Red Stag, but it doesn't come easy. Dense fog, steep terrain, and thick vegetation make finding a mature stag no easy task. Watch as Mark Peterson and his guide cover the gorgeous New Zealand landscape in search of one of the most sought-after big game animals on the South Island of New Zealand. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson is in Ethiopia hunting Mountain Nyala. After the first two days, Mark has taken a Menelik’s Bushbuck and a Colobus Monkey but no Mountain Nyala. Finally, on Day 3 the hunting party receives the green light from the military to hunt in the area they wanted. It's time to put the Gunwerks rifle to the test.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson continues his quest on the Hearst Ranch in California. Next up on the list, California Aoudad. These Aoudad were introduced to the ranch in the early 1920s and have thrived ever since.

programme.title: The Bearded Buck
programme.desc: Jerry and Austin head to Nebraska for an early season whitetail velvet hunt, something they've never done before! They're joined by Badlands Marketing Manager, Blake Vantussenbrook, in hopes of getting him on his biggest buck ever!

programme.title: American Heroes Outdoors
programme.desc: On this adventure we travel to Colorado and hook up with an organization called "Angling with officers" and we jump in the boat with some courageous law enforcement officers from local departments. We also honor the Rapid City, SD SCHEELS Hometown Hero, David Pearcy!

programme.title: American Heroes Outdoors
programme.desc: On this episode, we travel to Missouri River near Chamberlain, SD and connect with the South Dakota chapter of Paralyzed Veterans of America for the Joel Niemeyer "Gone Fishing" event. We get to guide and share the story of 100 year old WW2 Veteran Kenneth Carlson and his family. We also honor our next Rochester, MN Scheels Hometown Hero, Dustin Draeger!

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: An Honor No One Wants: A Gold Star Family is the immediate family member(s) of a fallen soldier who died while serving in a time of conflict. Sheila Mitchell-Murphy honors the memory her Son…American hero Etienne Murphy.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: 82 year old Navy veteran with more than 2.7 million TikTok followers, Patriotic Kenny talks of how he helps other vets through him gifting mobility scooters to fellow veterans in need.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Mike is back after em in Alabama and finally finds a tone that this ol gobbler seems to like. Some timely calling and waiting patiently for this longbeard and his hen to make their way to his setup pays off. Back to work Mike, we have calls to make! It's the WoodHaven Way!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: This 10 gauge doesn't mess around! Greg Keeton was a well deserved Prostaffer of the Year for WoodHaven. He joins Mark Scroggins and Tony Doty in Nebraska to hunt Merriam's and Rios. Greg has one of the very first Real Hen box calls and guess what, she still sings a pretty turkey tune!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Mike and Billy are changing things up a bit and head to South Georgia to hunt longbeards with the guys from In the South Outdoors. Anytime you get in the turkey woods with Bill Yargus, you are guaranteed to hear some jam up calling. It takes a couple of different setups but they finally get in this ol gobblers "bubble". Oh, did we mention he's a stud!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: World Champion caller Billy Yargus is hunting his home state of Missouri. Billy is a student of the outdoors and spends countless hours calling and observing birds in the off season. He has a good bead on several gobblers on the backside of the farm but as we all know, turkeys are going to be turkeys. A new setup on a mid-morning gobbler, some timely calling and a lot of patience payoff big for Billy. Trust your scouting info and your gut.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Mike is hunting public land in Alabama and has birds gobbling first thing. Listening and watching how this gobbler responds to calling and interacts with the hens with him, Mike relies on good ol experience to know how to setup to kill this longbeard.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Jeff Danker of Buckventures, returns to his home state of Oklahoma to chase a legendary buck on the Wallahchie Ranch — the "Big 8". With his bow in hand and one specific target in mind, the crew sets up in a blind for what turns into one of the craziest hunts they've ever experienced.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Young Captain Reed Tudor hosts Captain Mike in Duck Key, FL and puts him on Micro Tarpon, Redfish and Goliath Grouper.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Captain Mike travels to the "Forgotten Coast" to fish Keaton Beach for the first time with the legendary Captain Greg DeVault.

programme.title: Goin' Coastal
programme.desc: Join Captain Josh and Sheriff Eric Aden for a day of fishing and fun in Destin, Florida. These two friends hit the water, catching fish both offshore and inshore, and reeling in some impressive catches. With plenty of laughs, great stories, and a few surprises along the way, this episode is all about the joy of fishing and the bond between two guys who love the sea.

programme.title: Goin' Coastal
programme.desc: Fishing with the Mayor Description: Join the Major of destin as he ventures into the world of coastal fishing, leaving the city behind for a day of fun and unexpected challenges. With plenty of laughs and surprising twists, this episode showcases the Mayor in a whole new light.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Tanner and CA put their power fishing skills to the ultimate test.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA heads to Jupiter to fish with Ryan Nitz for giant snook.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA revisits some of the best moments of the year and some never before seen footage!

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Ca heads to Murrells Inlet, SC for some low country lure slingin'. What's on your menu? Shrimp and grits or Flounder?

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Rooted in history, Apalachicola, Florida, is where Capt. Jonathan Moss finds himself today, scouting the marsh for hungry backcountry dwellers.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: With only a limited amount of time, Capt. Jonathan Moss is in Pine Island Sound, on the hunt for monster migratory Tarpon.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: The tide is right for Capt. Jonathan Moss to chase that Northeast Florida slam.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Capt. Jonathan Moss is fishing in the Northern Indian River Lagoon with DOA Lures Founder, Capt. Mark Nichols.

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: We kick off Season 2 in Tennessee where we stop in Nashville, Townsend, and Johnson City!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through the mountains of western North Carolina, where we stop in Asheville and Cashiers!!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: David Lindsey’s 2024 Iowa season was anything but ordinary. He set his sights on one of the greatest bucks ever to roam The Lindsey Way farm — a deer he called “Pretty Boy.” From close calls to wild twists, David’s pursuit was full of highs, lows, and more excitement than any hunt in recent memory. But somewhere in the chaos, another story unfolded — a tough old buck that survived EHD and became just a little too predictable. David seized the opportunity, made the shot, and finally got some blood under his belt for the season.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture. Show host, Dan Cabela, participates in a scientific sampling project to monitor the population's health, and get first-hand experience of the relationship between local people and this arctic icon.

programme.title: HuntStand Presents
programme.desc: For decades, Paul Butski has set the standard in turkey calling. His name is etched into the NWTF Grand National Calling Championships Hall of Fame, and his influence stretches far beyond the stage. He didn’t just win competitions, he shaped the way we talk to turkeys – and more importantly, the way we hunt them.

programme.title: Pure Hunting
programme.desc: Willi and Chris are back in New Mexico, bowhunting elk. Willi made a poor shot on a bull, and after discovering the cause of the poor shot, he hopes to get redemption as their hunting days come to a close.

programme.title: Pure Hunting
programme.desc: Willi learns a black bear was on the carcass of the bull elk he had shot just days before. He hopes to sneak into the carcass on his Bakcou Scooters for a chance to shoot the bear.

programme.title: Bearded Buck
programme.desc: In this episode, Jerry and Austin are still in Nebraska hunting whitetails and muleys. After Jerry filled the first mule deer tag of the trip, the weather takes a turn for the worst and the deer movement slows down drastically. With just five days left in the trip to come up with a new game plan to figure out these whitetails and Austin's number one target mule deer, Jerry and Austin are worried that they may leave without filling another tag.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: It’s still opening week of bow season in Oklahoma and the crew has three tags left to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy has been playing cat and mouse with a buck called “The Kansas 8” and after 2 encounters he is trying to seal the coffin on this big Missouri whitetail once and for all.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton has had an amazing spring so far - almost too good to be true. You won't believe what happened on his latest turkey hunt in Georgia.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy is at the Raven working on some Big Tine clover maintenance and getting the plot he killed the Boss 8 in during rifle season ready to go. Tune in and get some tips for your clover plots and watch Randy take out the oldest deer on the Raven.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Every season, just like clockwork, mission-driven longboards gobble their way across the plains of South Dakota, the cornfields of Iowa, the timbered ridges of Michigan and the cattle ground of Kansas looking for their soulmates in one of the most celebrated hunting seasons in America…the wild turkey breeding season. Join Team Winchester as they stalk, call, flank and roost boss gobblers into range from coast to coast.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Mark Kayser breaks down how to read elevation lines, hidden saddles, and thermal hubs to outsmart deer. Then, we head to the rugged breaks of Wyoming to see these strategies put to the test.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark is wrapping up his Argentina hunt and heading to Entre Rios to track down the final two birds on his list: the Rock Pigeon and the Eared Dove. This area is home to over 10 million birds, making it a prime hunting ground. With flocks of pigeons and doves filling the skies, Mark is set for a fast-paced, action-packed finish to his hunt—a perfect end to an unforgettable adventure.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: This hunt tells a story in two parts. The first chapter follows Earl Peterson, Mark’s dad, as he steps into the field with one specific goal in mind. Experience, patience, and timing all play a role as the morning unfolds, setting the tone for the rest of the adventure. The second chapter shifts to Mark’s turn. On the coldest morning of the trip so far, the team heads into black-faced impala country. Wind, shifting conditions, and sharp eyes keep things interesting as they cover ground and look for the right opportunity. Every move matters, and nothing comes easy.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Each year we get to spend time in camp with some of our best friends. Zach from Big & J gets some time in a blind while reflecting on what hunting in Texas means to him. Texas provides a wide variety of game, and chasing these creatures all across the state is some of the best times had.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: From time to time you have moments that will change the way think about something. That is what happened for us while hunting the Scimitar Oryx with Republic Outdoors. Hunting is more than harvesting animals, it’s conservation, and in this story, we learn how a species nearly extinct gets another chance to live.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode Slade takes Bentley on his very first duck hunt. Slade also gets to take a trip to duck hunting paradise with some great buddies of his. This episode is all about having a great time with great friends.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Season 4 of Hunting Land Man is starting off with a bang. In this episode, it’s opening week of turkey season in Mississippi, and these birds are doing it right. Cool mornings, gobbles breaking daylight, and longbeards fired up and on the move. We are fired up for the 2026 turkey season.

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: Slade and the old Trained Assassins crew are headed back to Montrose in pursuit of a big elk. The conditions are not favorable but the crew is sticking it out!

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: TA Mahoney is a classic mom and pop store known for going the extra mile and treating everyday people like family. Keep an eye on Mike though, you never know what kind of prank is up his sleeve.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Josh and his wife Anna own the most prolific tackle shop in Fort Walton Beach. Josh's family eats, sleeps and breathes fishing.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Meet just a few of the amazing crew members of the Old Salt Board of Directors. This nonprofit is more than just a fishing group. They make a difference in their community.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Kylee Short, an Orlando based bass angler, shares how she discovered fishing later in life. This LBP Spotlight highlights her journey on the water and strong family support.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: On this weeks episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade and his daughter Asa take on the turkey woods together for the first time. As this is Asa's first time turkey hunting some quick pre hunt preparations are in order. Slade also was able to sit down with a good friend and registered forester to talk fire and the benefits of fire in land management. The next day, Asa and Slade were able to connect with a thundering tom as Asa punched her first ever turkey tag!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Slade and Caleb head back to Missouri to catch the end of bow season,and the start of rifle. Slade arrows a great buck (twice), and we preview the latest property Slade & Co. purchased in MO.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Opening day in Mississippi has arrived and Slade is taking full advantage of it! With the weather not cooperating Slade and Zeb stumble into a turkey hunt they won't soon forget. Two days later, the hot streak continues with Slade connecting on another turkey on an 83 acre property he has for sale! Also, Slade gives some tips and his ideas on small acreage hunting, and how it can be just as lucrative as big tracks of land!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade and his son take on the turkey woods for an afternoon hunt. Although the woods remained quiet, a tom soon snuck out of the woods and on the end of Bentley's barrel! After that Slade was able to help a client of his harvest his first turkey! And boy was it an action packed hunt! Jakes, hens, and tons of gobbles, This hunt is spectacular! Lastly, Slade introduces a property that we have already made a video on, but with just how amazing the land is, we had to include it! Floweree is the epitome of a sportsman's paradise!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: On this episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade, Ryan and Bearcub take on the midwest! After some tough hunting, the group is able to make a move to get in position on a BIG midwestern gobbler! Bearcub was able to harvest him after working the bird for almost 45 minutes! Slade and Ryan also do some Pre-Deer season work, and establish where the fall food-plots will be!

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade and his brother Tai take us around a new investment property - a 55 acre tract in Wilkinson County, MS and go over what it took to take an undesirable raw tract, to a place with multiple offers on the first day it was put on the market.

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through Georgia, where we stop in Helen, Alpharetta and Atlanta!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through South Carolina, where we stop in Clemson, Travelers Rest and Charleston!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through Florida, where we stop in Jacksonville, Jupiter, Boca Raton and Miami!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through Florida, where we stop in Key West, Marathon, Bonita Springs, and Sanibel Island.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This spring, our crew was able to dive into some turkey hunting at the G2 ranch with long time TTHA Members Mike Reeber and Dan Marcasol. After a slow start, the crew was finally able to connect on a great Rio Tom and a few hogs. Learning and sharing the stories of TTHA members is something we strive to do each year.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This is the story of how FE Hill ranch got it's start. From its inception, FE Hill has created opportunities for people to experience the outdoors and create memories. For us, it has created a tradition of Fathers and Sons hunting together.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Follow TTHA President/CEO Christina Pittman and her husband Matt this week as they chase Whitetails at Morrow Ranch. This is a hunt that they had purchased earlier at the Disabled Outdoorsman in the year to be able to spend some time in the woods. A few days away to relax and spend quality time with loved ones should always be a priority. Morrow Ranch is definitely a go to place for low fence Texas hunting.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Denise Sanchez was awarded a hunt at G2 following our Hunters Extravaganza drawings. Its great to get to spend time on the ranch with new members of TTHA. Denise gets familiar with a giant and learns about new technology while on this hunt.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We love our members. We spent some time in camp with Ron Chandler. Ron Recently renewed his TTHA Membership in 2022 and was awarded a member hunt for 2023. We spend time reflecting on what hunting means to Ron and spending time in camp at G2 with hunters alike.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: WE join Bobby Falkenberg as he joins Bret Ferguson from Double T Outfitters. Bobby is an avid outdoorsman, judge, and father of 5. Bret manages 30,000 acres in Ozona TX, the knowledge he has of every inch of this amazing low fence ranch is remarkable. Bobby has an action packed 2 day hunt.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Over the years we have been able to hunt with a lot of amazing people. This episode takes us to Las Palmas ranch to hunt with Payton Angelle. Payton was awarded this hunt from the amazing people over at Sky High for kids.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: The bond between and parent and child can grow in many ways, but nothing compares to the bond shared when hunting. The annual Father-Son hunt at FE Hill Outfitters has becoming a tradition for Heath and his son Gavin and in 2021, Gavin was able to take his largest buck to date, with his dad Heath behind the camera.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Coastal Conservation Association Florida Star Tournament redfish releases.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads down to Islamorada to get some deep water groceries with the Starbrite team.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads out to the very first spot we ever filmed a BWO show, with the first guide ever on BWO Capt. John Beach. This time they're catching sheepshead, hopefully nobody gets bit this time.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads to the great state of Alaska to bring you a sight never seen before on this show, Capt. Blair in close-toed shoes. He catches some petty cool fish too, thanks to Capt. Geoff Peck at Baranof Fishing Excursions.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff dive into the wild waters off Hawaii’s coast, coming face-to-face with sharks and graceful manta rays. The adrenaline peaks when Kyle takes a heart-pounding leap off a towering 40-foot cliff, making this adventure as daring as it is unforgettable.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff explore the breathtaking Waipio Valley, the Hawaiian "Valley of the Kings," in search of prawns and bullfrogs. Their adventure continues up a massive volcano, hunting invasive rams, before ending with a feast of native fish paired with island fruits and spices.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff hear about the dangers of fishing for Bluefin Tuna in the Atlantic Ocean from locals in Gloucester, Massachusetts. Then they head 30 miles out onto the open ocean with Darrin of Reel Respect Charters in the hopes of landing their own giant.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff team up with their friends from Boss Shotshells in South Dakota for a high-energy upland hunt, pursuing wild pheasants with the help of skilled hunting dogs. The adventure takes a quirky turn with a visit to the iconic Corn Palace.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: We were honored to be a part of the Gold Star Youth Outdoors Turkey Hunt at Iron Horse Farms in Alabama. Cole Barthel and Bryan Brown were able to get Charlie Willington his first turkey. Be sure to check out Gold Star Youth Outdoors and Iron Horse Farms.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Realtree's David Blanton heads south of the border to hunt the Gould's turkey with Jay Scott Outdoors. For more info on hunting with Jay Scott Outdoors: visit www.gouldsturkeyhunt.com

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Second leg in the slam is complete! Justin Webb killed his Rio in Texas. Next stop - Tennessee for the Eastern.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Roger Culpepper takes Sophia Mahlke and her dad Tom to kill her first longbeard. The guys and girl find the gobblers on roost in a beautiful Georgia hardwood bottom and make their move.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Low turkey population densities and the rocky, steep terrain of New York State's Adirondack Mountains create a unique spring turkey hunting challenge only found in this region of the country. It's not always easy to find wild turkeys here, but when you can, there is always the chance that this bird has never been hunted making success particularly special.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: The Spring Thunder crew teams up with the guys from Hunt Club and Realtree Roadtrips to take a group of veterans on a life changing turkey hunt at Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, AL.

programme.title: TRP
programme.desc: GET THE MOST OUT OF ACTIVETARGET 2How can you get the MOST out of your forward facing sonar?!Jacob Wheeler earns his living by using ActiveTarget 2. He is a highly decorated professional bass fisherman in the MLF, including TWO back-to-back Angler of the Year honors. He uses forward facing sonar every single day. programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: We listen to guides and anglers on how they attempt to hook into the migrating tarpon that pass thru PCB’s beautiful beach waters each summer. It’s a lesson in humility, respect and yes....luck.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Capt. Justin Leake and a fellow guide search for redfish along the beach, but Mother Nature has other ideas and gives them a show they didn’t expect.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: For Capt. Justin Leake, sight fishing the beach is one of the most productive, and FUN ways to fish. When that doesn’t work, Capt. Todd Jones suggests fishing an artificial reef, and as it turns out, that’s where dinner lives.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Meredith McCord arrives in late spring and joins Capt. Justin Leake on the hunt for a fish she’s never caught before. Lo and behold, it’s a day fit for a king.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Big horn sheep are one of North America’s most spectacular animals, but future conservation requires large-scale, hands-on efforts. Joining a multi-agency translocation in Texas, Dan Cabela is in the thick of it.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: In the sparsely populated country of Namibia, in southern Africa, Dan Cabela visits conservationist Robin Hurt to see how private land ownership and hunting have restored the landscape to a thriving wildlife area.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: This episode is all about thanking our service men and women who have served or are still currently serving; Our pals Wes Thomas and Terry McWilliams join in to help thank these heroes, along with great companies like IN 811 and Centerpoint; special thanks to West Bogg Park for having us again this year.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: This week we join professional angler, Andrew Nordbye, for a cold day on Guntersville to catch some giant crappie; traveling through a major winter storm and arriving at an abandoned lake was the not-surprising scene for the day. Andrew's priorities are in order when it comes to his marriage, his faith, and his family, including just-arrived new babies...but he sill made time to squeeze in this day on water with us!

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: The sharks won’t stop Capt. Jared and special Guest Capt. Jimmy Hendricks from having their day.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Rich's son Reed, who takes the wheel and directs the team into some amazing early morning baby tarpon fishing in the Everglades.

programme.title: saltwater experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor take Hunter Smallback into the Everglades for some snook fishing, and the action really heats up when some hungry Lemon sharks crash the party.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson is in Ethiopia hunting Mountain Nyala. After the first two days, Mark has taken a Menelik’s Bushbuck and a Colobus Monkey but no Mountain Nyala. Finally, on Day 3 the hunting party receives the green light from the military to hunt in the area they wanted. It's time to put the Gunwerks rifle to the test.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson continues his quest on the Hearst Ranch in California. Next up on the list, California Aoudad. These Aoudad were introduced to the ranch in the early 1920s and have thrived ever since.

programme.title: The Bearded Buck
programme.desc: Jerry and Austin head to Nebraska for an early season whitetail velvet hunt, something they've never done before! They're joined by Badlands Marketing Manager, Blake Vantussenbrook, in hopes of getting him on his biggest buck ever!

programme.title: American Heroes Outdoors
programme.desc: On this adventure we travel to Colorado and hook up with an organization called "Angling with officers" and we jump in the boat with some courageous law enforcement officers from local departments. We also honor the Rapid City, SD SCHEELS Hometown Hero, David Pearcy!

programme.title: American Heroes Outdoors
programme.desc: On this episode, we travel to Missouri River near Chamberlain, SD and connect with the South Dakota chapter of Paralyzed Veterans of America for the Joel Niemeyer "Gone Fishing" event. We get to guide and share the story of 100 year old WW2 Veteran Kenneth Carlson and his family. We also honor our next Rochester, MN Scheels Hometown Hero, Dustin Draeger!

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: An Honor No One Wants: A Gold Star Family is the immediate family member(s) of a fallen soldier who died while serving in a time of conflict. Sheila Mitchell-Murphy honors the memory her Son…American hero Etienne Murphy.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: 82 year old Navy veteran with more than 2.7 million TikTok followers, Patriotic Kenny talks of how he helps other vets through him gifting mobility scooters to fellow veterans in need.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Mike is back after em in Alabama and finally finds a tone that this ol gobbler seems to like. Some timely calling and waiting patiently for this longbeard and his hen to make their way to his setup pays off. Back to work Mike, we have calls to make! It's the WoodHaven Way!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: This 10 gauge doesn't mess around! Greg Keeton was a well deserved Prostaffer of the Year for WoodHaven. He joins Mark Scroggins and Tony Doty in Nebraska to hunt Merriam's and Rios. Greg has one of the very first Real Hen box calls and guess what, she still sings a pretty turkey tune!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Mike and Billy are changing things up a bit and head to South Georgia to hunt longbeards with the guys from In the South Outdoors. Anytime you get in the turkey woods with Bill Yargus, you are guaranteed to hear some jam up calling. It takes a couple of different setups but they finally get in this ol gobblers "bubble". Oh, did we mention he's a stud!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: World Champion caller Billy Yargus is hunting his home state of Missouri. Billy is a student of the outdoors and spends countless hours calling and observing birds in the off season. He has a good bead on several gobblers on the backside of the farm but as we all know, turkeys are going to be turkeys. A new setup on a mid-morning gobbler, some timely calling and a lot of patience payoff big for Billy. Trust your scouting info and your gut.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Mike is hunting public land in Alabama and has birds gobbling first thing. Listening and watching how this gobbler responds to calling and interacts with the hens with him, Mike relies on good ol experience to know how to setup to kill this longbeard.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Jeff Danker of Buckventures, returns to his home state of Oklahoma to chase a legendary buck on the Wallahchie Ranch — the "Big 8". With his bow in hand and one specific target in mind, the crew sets up in a blind for what turns into one of the craziest hunts they've ever experienced.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Young Captain Reed Tudor hosts Captain Mike in Duck Key, FL and puts him on Micro Tarpon, Redfish and Goliath Grouper.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Captain Mike travels to the "Forgotten Coast" to fish Keaton Beach for the first time with the legendary Captain Greg DeVault.

programme.title: Goin' Coastal
programme.desc: Join Captain Josh and Sheriff Eric Aden for a day of fishing and fun in Destin, Florida. These two friends hit the water, catching fish both offshore and inshore, and reeling in some impressive catches. With plenty of laughs, great stories, and a few surprises along the way, this episode is all about the joy of fishing and the bond between two guys who love the sea.

programme.title: Goin' Coastal
programme.desc: Fishing with the Mayor Description: Join the Major of destin as he ventures into the world of coastal fishing, leaving the city behind for a day of fun and unexpected challenges. With plenty of laughs and surprising twists, this episode showcases the Mayor in a whole new light.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Tanner and CA put their power fishing skills to the ultimate test.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA heads to Jupiter to fish with Ryan Nitz for giant snook.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: CA revisits some of the best moments of the year and some never before seen footage!

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Ca heads to Murrells Inlet, SC for some low country lure slingin'. What's on your menu? Shrimp and grits or Flounder?

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Rooted in history, Apalachicola, Florida, is where Capt. Jonathan Moss finds himself today, scouting the marsh for hungry backcountry dwellers.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: With only a limited amount of time, Capt. Jonathan Moss is in Pine Island Sound, on the hunt for monster migratory Tarpon.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: The tide is right for Capt. Jonathan Moss to chase that Northeast Florida slam.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Capt. Jonathan Moss is fishing in the Northern Indian River Lagoon with DOA Lures Founder, Capt. Mark Nichols.

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: We kick off Season 2 in Tennessee where we stop in Nashville, Townsend, and Johnson City!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through the mountains of western North Carolina, where we stop in Asheville and Cashiers!!

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: David Lindsey’s 2024 Iowa season was anything but ordinary. He set his sights on one of the greatest bucks ever to roam The Lindsey Way farm — a deer he called “Pretty Boy.” From close calls to wild twists, David’s pursuit was full of highs, lows, and more excitement than any hunt in recent memory. But somewhere in the chaos, another story unfolded — a tough old buck that survived EHD and became just a little too predictable. David seized the opportunity, made the shot, and finally got some blood under his belt for the season.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture. Show host, Dan Cabela, participates in a scientific sampling project to monitor the population's health, and get first-hand experience of the relationship between local people and this arctic icon.

programme.title: HuntStand Presents
programme.desc: For decades, Paul Butski has set the standard in turkey calling. His name is etched into the NWTF Grand National Calling Championships Hall of Fame, and his influence stretches far beyond the stage. He didn’t just win competitions, he shaped the way we talk to turkeys – and more importantly, the way we hunt them.

programme.title: Pure Hunting
programme.desc: Willi and Chris are back in New Mexico, bowhunting elk. Willi made a poor shot on a bull, and after discovering the cause of the poor shot, he hopes to get redemption as their hunting days come to a close.

programme.title: Pure Hunting
programme.desc: Willi learns a black bear was on the carcass of the bull elk he had shot just days before. He hopes to sneak into the carcass on his Bakcou Scooters for a chance to shoot the bear.

programme.title: Bearded Buck
programme.desc: In this episode, Jerry and Austin are still in Nebraska hunting whitetails and muleys. After Jerry filled the first mule deer tag of the trip, the weather takes a turn for the worst and the deer movement slows down drastically. With just five days left in the trip to come up with a new game plan to figure out these whitetails and Austin's number one target mule deer, Jerry and Austin are worried that they may leave without filling another tag.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: It’s still opening week of bow season in Oklahoma and the crew has three tags left to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy has been playing cat and mouse with a buck called “The Kansas 8” and after 2 encounters he is trying to seal the coffin on this big Missouri whitetail once and for all.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: David Blanton has had an amazing spring so far - almost too good to be true. You won't believe what happened on his latest turkey hunt in Georgia.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy is at the Raven working on some Big Tine clover maintenance and getting the plot he killed the Boss 8 in during rifle season ready to go. Tune in and get some tips for your clover plots and watch Randy take out the oldest deer on the Raven.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Every season, just like clockwork, mission-driven longboards gobble their way across the plains of South Dakota, the cornfields of Iowa, the timbered ridges of Michigan and the cattle ground of Kansas looking for their soulmates in one of the most celebrated hunting seasons in America…the wild turkey breeding season. Join Team Winchester as they stalk, call, flank and roost boss gobblers into range from coast to coast.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Mark Kayser breaks down how to read elevation lines, hidden saddles, and thermal hubs to outsmart deer. Then, we head to the rugged breaks of Wyoming to see these strategies put to the test.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark is wrapping up his Argentina hunt and heading to Entre Rios to track down the final two birds on his list: the Rock Pigeon and the Eared Dove. This area is home to over 10 million birds, making it a prime hunting ground. With flocks of pigeons and doves filling the skies, Mark is set for a fast-paced, action-packed finish to his hunt—a perfect end to an unforgettable adventure.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: This hunt tells a story in two parts. The first chapter follows Earl Peterson, Mark’s dad, as he steps into the field with one specific goal in mind. Experience, patience, and timing all play a role as the morning unfolds, setting the tone for the rest of the adventure. The second chapter shifts to Mark’s turn. On the coldest morning of the trip so far, the team heads into black-faced impala country. Wind, shifting conditions, and sharp eyes keep things interesting as they cover ground and look for the right opportunity. Every move matters, and nothing comes easy.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Each year we get to spend time in camp with some of our best friends. Zach from Big & J gets some time in a blind while reflecting on what hunting in Texas means to him. Texas provides a wide variety of game, and chasing these creatures all across the state is some of the best times had.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: From time to time you have moments that will change the way think about something. That is what happened for us while hunting the Scimitar Oryx with Republic Outdoors. Hunting is more than harvesting animals, it’s conservation, and in this story, we learn how a species nearly extinct gets another chance to live.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode Slade takes Bentley on his very first duck hunt. Slade also gets to take a trip to duck hunting paradise with some great buddies of his. This episode is all about having a great time with great friends.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Season 4 of Hunting Land Man is starting off with a bang. In this episode, it’s opening week of turkey season in Mississippi, and these birds are doing it right. Cool mornings, gobbles breaking daylight, and longbeards fired up and on the move. We are fired up for the 2026 turkey season.

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: Slade and the old Trained Assassins crew are headed back to Montrose in pursuit of a big elk. The conditions are not favorable but the crew is sticking it out!

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: TA Mahoney is a classic mom and pop store known for going the extra mile and treating everyday people like family. Keep an eye on Mike though, you never know what kind of prank is up his sleeve.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Josh and his wife Anna own the most prolific tackle shop in Fort Walton Beach. Josh's family eats, sleeps and breathes fishing.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Meet just a few of the amazing crew members of the Old Salt Board of Directors. This nonprofit is more than just a fishing group. They make a difference in their community.

programme.title: LBP Spotlight
programme.desc: Kylee Short, an Orlando based bass angler, shares how she discovered fishing later in life. This LBP Spotlight highlights her journey on the water and strong family support.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: On this weeks episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade and his daughter Asa take on the turkey woods together for the first time. As this is Asa's first time turkey hunting some quick pre hunt preparations are in order. Slade also was able to sit down with a good friend and registered forester to talk fire and the benefits of fire in land management. The next day, Asa and Slade were able to connect with a thundering tom as Asa punched her first ever turkey tag!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Slade and Caleb head back to Missouri to catch the end of bow season,and the start of rifle. Slade arrows a great buck (twice), and we preview the latest property Slade & Co. purchased in MO.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Opening day in Mississippi has arrived and Slade is taking full advantage of it! With the weather not cooperating Slade and Zeb stumble into a turkey hunt they won't soon forget. Two days later, the hot streak continues with Slade connecting on another turkey on an 83 acre property he has for sale! Also, Slade gives some tips and his ideas on small acreage hunting, and how it can be just as lucrative as big tracks of land!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade and his son take on the turkey woods for an afternoon hunt. Although the woods remained quiet, a tom soon snuck out of the woods and on the end of Bentley's barrel! After that Slade was able to help a client of his harvest his first turkey! And boy was it an action packed hunt! Jakes, hens, and tons of gobbles, This hunt is spectacular! Lastly, Slade introduces a property that we have already made a video on, but with just how amazing the land is, we had to include it! Floweree is the epitome of a sportsman's paradise!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: On this episode of Hunting Land Man, Slade, Ryan and Bearcub take on the midwest! After some tough hunting, the group is able to make a move to get in position on a BIG midwestern gobbler! Bearcub was able to harvest him after working the bird for almost 45 minutes! Slade and Ryan also do some Pre-Deer season work, and establish where the fall food-plots will be!

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade and his brother Tai take us around a new investment property - a 55 acre tract in Wilkinson County, MS and go over what it took to take an undesirable raw tract, to a place with multiple offers on the first day it was put on the market.

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through Georgia, where we stop in Helen, Alpharetta and Atlanta!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through South Carolina, where we stop in Clemson, Travelers Rest and Charleston!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through Florida, where we stop in Jacksonville, Jupiter, Boca Raton and Miami!!

programme.title: Fly Shop Tour
programme.desc: This episode we make our way through Florida, where we stop in Key West, Marathon, Bonita Springs, and Sanibel Island.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This spring, our crew was able to dive into some turkey hunting at the G2 ranch with long time TTHA Members Mike Reeber and Dan Marcasol. After a slow start, the crew was finally able to connect on a great Rio Tom and a few hogs. Learning and sharing the stories of TTHA members is something we strive to do each year.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This is the story of how FE Hill ranch got it's start. From its inception, FE Hill has created opportunities for people to experience the outdoors and create memories. For us, it has created a tradition of Fathers and Sons hunting together.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Follow TTHA President/CEO Christina Pittman and her husband Matt this week as they chase Whitetails at Morrow Ranch. This is a hunt that they had purchased earlier at the Disabled Outdoorsman in the year to be able to spend some time in the woods. A few days away to relax and spend quality time with loved ones should always be a priority. Morrow Ranch is definitely a go to place for low fence Texas hunting.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Denise Sanchez was awarded a hunt at G2 following our Hunters Extravaganza drawings. Its great to get to spend time on the ranch with new members of TTHA. Denise gets familiar with a giant and learns about new technology while on this hunt.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We love our members. We spent some time in camp with Ron Chandler. Ron Recently renewed his TTHA Membership in 2022 and was awarded a member hunt for 2023. We spend time reflecting on what hunting means to Ron and spending time in camp at G2 with hunters alike.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: WE join Bobby Falkenberg as he joins Bret Ferguson from Double T Outfitters. Bobby is an avid outdoorsman, judge, and father of 5. Bret manages 30,000 acres in Ozona TX, the knowledge he has of every inch of this amazing low fence ranch is remarkable. Bobby has an action packed 2 day hunt.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Over the years we have been able to hunt with a lot of amazing people. This episode takes us to Las Palmas ranch to hunt with Payton Angelle. Payton was awarded this hunt from the amazing people over at Sky High for kids.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: The bond between and parent and child can grow in many ways, but nothing compares to the bond shared when hunting. The annual Father-Son hunt at FE Hill Outfitters has becoming a tradition for Heath and his son Gavin and in 2021, Gavin was able to take his largest buck to date, with his dad Heath behind the camera.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Coastal Conservation Association Florida Star Tournament redfish releases.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads down to Islamorada to get some deep water groceries with the Starbrite team.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads out to the very first spot we ever filmed a BWO show, with the first guide ever on BWO Capt. John Beach. This time they're catching sheepshead, hopefully nobody gets bit this time.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads to the great state of Alaska to bring you a sight never seen before on this show, Capt. Blair in close-toed shoes. He catches some petty cool fish too, thanks to Capt. Geoff Peck at Baranof Fishing Excursions.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff dive into the wild waters off Hawaii’s coast, coming face-to-face with sharks and graceful manta rays. The adrenaline peaks when Kyle takes a heart-pounding leap off a towering 40-foot cliff, making this adventure as daring as it is unforgettable.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff explore the breathtaking Waipio Valley, the Hawaiian "Valley of the Kings," in search of prawns and bullfrogs. Their adventure continues up a massive volcano, hunting invasive rams, before ending with a feast of native fish paired with island fruits and spices.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff hear about the dangers of fishing for Bluefin Tuna in the Atlantic Ocean from locals in Gloucester, Massachusetts. Then they head 30 miles out onto the open ocean with Darrin of Reel Respect Charters in the hopes of landing their own giant.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle and Jeff team up with their friends from Boss Shotshells in South Dakota for a high-energy upland hunt, pursuing wild pheasants with the help of skilled hunting dogs. The adventure takes a quirky turn with a visit to the iconic Corn Palace.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: We were honored to be a part of the Gold Star Youth Outdoors Turkey Hunt at Iron Horse Farms in Alabama. Cole Barthel and Bryan Brown were able to get Charlie Willington his first turkey. Be sure to check out Gold Star Youth Outdoors and Iron Horse Farms.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Realtree's David Blanton heads south of the border to hunt the Gould's turkey with Jay Scott Outdoors. For more info on hunting with Jay Scott Outdoors: visit www.gouldsturkeyhunt.com

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Second leg in the slam is complete! Justin Webb killed his Rio in Texas. Next stop - Tennessee for the Eastern.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Roger Culpepper takes Sophia Mahlke and her dad Tom to kill her first longbeard. The guys and girl find the gobblers on roost in a beautiful Georgia hardwood bottom and make their move.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Low turkey population densities and the rocky, steep terrain of New York State's Adirondack Mountains create a unique spring turkey hunting challenge only found in this region of the country. It's not always easy to find wild turkeys here, but when you can, there is always the chance that this bird has never been hunted making success particularly special.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: The Spring Thunder crew teams up with the guys from Hunt Club and Realtree Roadtrips to take a group of veterans on a life changing turkey hunt at Redstone Arsenal in Huntsville, AL.

programme.title: TRP
programme.desc: GET THE MOST OUT OF ACTIVETARGET 2How can you get the MOST out of your forward facing sonar?!Jacob Wheeler earns his living by using ActiveTarget 2. He is a highly decorated professional bass fisherman in the MLF, including TWO back-to-back Angler of the Year honors. He uses forward facing sonar every single day. programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: We listen to guides and anglers on how they attempt to hook into the migrating tarpon that pass thru PCB’s beautiful beach waters each summer. It’s a lesson in humility, respect and yes....luck.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Capt. Justin Leake and a fellow guide search for redfish along the beach, but Mother Nature has other ideas and gives them a show they didn’t expect.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: For Capt. Justin Leake, sight fishing the beach is one of the most productive, and FUN ways to fish. When that doesn’t work, Capt. Todd Jones suggests fishing an artificial reef, and as it turns out, that’s where dinner lives.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Meredith McCord arrives in late spring and joins Capt. Justin Leake on the hunt for a fish she’s never caught before. Lo and behold, it’s a day fit for a king.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Big horn sheep are one of North America’s most spectacular animals, but future conservation requires large-scale, hands-on efforts. Joining a multi-agency translocation in Texas, Dan Cabela is in the thick of it.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: In the sparsely populated country of Namibia, in southern Africa, Dan Cabela visits conservationist Robin Hurt to see how private land ownership and hunting have restored the landscape to a thriving wildlife area.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: This episode is all about thanking our service men and women who have served or are still currently serving; Our pals Wes Thomas and Terry McWilliams join in to help thank these heroes, along with great companies like IN 811 and Centerpoint; special thanks to West Bogg Park for having us again this year.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: This week we join professional angler, Andrew Nordbye, for a cold day on Guntersville to catch some giant crappie; traveling through a major winter storm and arriving at an abandoned lake was the not-surprising scene for the day. Andrew's priorities are in order when it comes to his marriage, his faith, and his family, including just-arrived new babies...but he sill made time to squeeze in this day on water with us!

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: The sharks won’t stop Capt. Jared and special Guest Capt. Jimmy Hendricks from having their day.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Rich's son Reed, who takes the wheel and directs the team into some amazing early morning baby tarpon fishing in the Everglades.

programme.title: saltwater experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor take Hunter Smallback into the Everglades for some snook fishing, and the action really heats up when some hungry Lemon sharks crash the party.

programme.desc: Feeling responsible for his father's death, which occurred during a climbing expedition, Peter Garrett (Chris O'Donnell) has quit the pursuit in favor of photography, while his sister, Annie (Robin Tunney), has become a top climber. Facing his fears, Peter assembles a rescue team, including experienced climber Montgomery Wick (Scott Glenn), to save them.

programme.title: The Principal

programme.title: The 7th Voyage of Sinbad
programme.desc: The heroic mariner must clear all sorts of hurdles in the way of rescuing a princess who’s been miniaturized and held captive by a ruthless magician.

programme.title: Born Yesterday
programme.desc: Brassy blonde moll Billie Dawn (Judy Holliday) hits Washington, D.C., with her unscrupulous millionaire sugar daddy, Harry Brock (Broderick Crawford), and his sleazy lawyer, Jim Devery (Howard St. John), who has been pressuring Harry to marry Billie by pointing out that a wife cannot be forced to testify against her husband. In an effort to make Billie more socially acceptable, Harry hires journalist Paul Verrall (William Holden) to smarten her up -- and sparks soon fly between the pair.

programme.title: Don't Knock the Twist
programme.desc: Dancers prepare for a TV special on the twist in this musical featuring performances of chart-topping hits from the heyday of rock 'n' roll.

programme.desc: An artist hopes to move to Paris, but his psychiatrist fiancée fears abandoning her patients, so he dons various disguises to cure the lonesome women.

programme.title: Our Wife
programme.desc: A composer (Melvyn Douglas) goes on a cruise and meets a woman (Ruth Hussey) he likes more than his wife (Ellen Drew).

programme.title: Cactus Flower
programme.desc: A dentist pretends he's married to avoid committing to his girlfriend. When she asks to meet his wife, he enlists his nurse to pose as his spouse.

programme.title: In Cold Blood
programme.desc: Based on the book by Truman Capote.
